steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The skill is punctuation conventions. The sentence opens a parenthetical with "(" before "as many as 200,000, by some ______" — the decisive clue is that every opening parenthesis must be matched by a closing parenthesis.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — estimates): Closes the parenthetical with ")" immediately after "estimates," correctly pairing with the opening "(" earlier in the sentence. The sentence then flows uninterrupted into "in a complex..." ✓
  • Choice B — estimates),: Closes the parenthetical correctly but adds an unnecessary comma between ")" and "in a complex..." — no comma is needed before a prepositional phrase that is essential to the sentence's meaning. ✗
  • Choice C: estimates —: Uses an em dash to close the element, but an em dash cannot be paired with an opening parenthesis — mismatched punctuation marks leave the parenthetical unclosed. ✗
  • Choice D — estimates: Provides no closing punctuation at all, leaving the opening parenthesis unmatched and the parenthetical element unclosed. ✗
That matches Choice A — estimates).
